headline: A claimed US export-control order forcing Anthropic to pull Claude Fable 5 dominates the cycle, alongside a wave of 1,000-plus tokens per second inference claims.
body: The recommended channels converge on reports that the US government ordered Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5, and in some accounts Mythos 5, citing export-control concerns and a claimed jailbreak vulnerability [2][4][5], with IBM's Mixture of Experts podcast also covering Fable 5's tier routing system and Apple's announced shift to cloud AI via an NVIDIA partnership at WWDC 2026 [7]. Separately, two speed claims land together: Xiaomi and TileRT's trillion-parameter MoE model hits over 1,000 tokens per second using FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime [1], while Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma reaches similar speeds by applying diffusion and bidirectional passes to text generation on H100s [3]. On tooling, IBM details using MCP and context engineering to align agents with design systems [0], and CopilotKit demonstrates generative UI that lets agents render React components with human-in-the-loop approval [6]. Non-recommended channels echo the Fable 5 shutdown [10][11][12] and add GPT-6 rumors framed against OpenAI's eroding position with Microsoft, Apple, and Google [13].
